devanagari: अथ षोडशोऽध्यायः । श्रीउद्धव उवाच । त्वं ब्रह्म परमं साक्षादनाद्यन्तमपावृतम् । सर्वेषामपि भावानां त्राणस्थित्यप्ययोद्भवः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha ṣoḍaśo'dhyāyaḥśrīuddhava uvācatvaṃ brahma paramaṃ sākṣādanādyantamapāvṛtamsarveṣāmapi bhāvānāṃ trāṇasthityapyayodbhavaḥ
translation:
Uddhava said:
You are the veritable Supreme Brahman having neither the beginning nor end. You are unobscured (by Māyā or its products). You are the Maker of creation, protection and destruction of all beings.

devanagari: उच्चावचेषु भूतेषु दुर्ज्ञेयमकृतात्मभिः । उपासते त्वां भगवन्याथातथ्येन ब्राह्मणाः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu durjñeyamakṛtātmabhiḥupāsate tvāṃ bhagavanyāthātathyena brāhmaṇāḥ
translation: Although you are very difficult to be cognised by persons who have not controlled their minds, O Lord, persons who have understood the real import of the Vedas propitiate you (by contemplating on you in rhe proper manner), in your real form as abiding in all beings high or low.

devanagari: येषु येषु च भूतेषु भक्त्या त्वां परमर्षयः । उपासीनाः प्रपद्यन्ते संसिद्धिं तद्वदस्व मे ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yeṣu yeṣu ca bhūteṣu bhaktyā tvāṃ paramarṣayaḥupāsīnāḥ prapadyante saṃsiddhiṃ tadvadasva me
translation: Pray, be pleased to narrate to me the various objects or beings (in which you have manifested yourself and[1]), by devoutly contemplating on you through which the great sages (of yore) have attained final beatitude.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
And the procedure of contemplating on you in those several objects and beings—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: गूढश्चरसि भूतात्मा भूतानां भूतभावन । न त्वां पश्यन्ति भूतानि पश्यन्तं मोहितानि ते ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: gūḍhaścarasi bhūtātmā bhūtānāṃ bhūtabhāvanana tvāṃ paśyanti bhūtāni paśyantaṃ mohitāni te
translation: You are the creator and Protector of beings. As their Inner Controller, you abide in them without being manifest to them. Being deluded by your Māyā, the beings cannot see you who, however, perceive them all.

devanagari: याः काश्च भूमौ दिवि वै रसायां विभूतयो दिक्षु महाविभूते । ता मह्यमाख्याह्यनुभावितास्ते नमामि ते तीर्थपदाङ्घ्रिपद्मम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yāḥ kāśca bhūmau divi vai rasāyāṃ vibhūtayo dikṣu mahāvibhūtetā mahyamākhyāhyanubhāvitāste namāmi te tīrthapadāṅghripadmam
translation: O Lord of infinite glories! Be pleased to describe unto me whatever glorious forms of yours have been vested by you with special powers and have been manifested on the earth, in the heavens, in the subterranean regions and in all the quarters. I bow to your lotus feet which are the abode of all sacred places.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एवमेतदहं पृष्टः प्रश्नं प्रश्नविदां वर । युयुत्सुना विनशने सपत्नैरर्जुनेन वै ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācaevametadahaṃ pṛṣṭaḥ praśnaṃ praśnavidāṃ varayuyutsunā vinaśane sapatnairarjunena vai
translation:
The glorious Lord said:
O Uddhava I You are the excellent one among persons who make (intelligent) queries (with proper understanding). A question similar to this very question was put to me by Arjuna who was eager to fight with the enemies, at Kurukṣetra.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Strictly speaking Vinaśana designates the spot in Kurukṣetra where the Sarasvatī disappeared (Mahābhārata Vana 82.111) but here the battlefield of Kurukṣetra in general is implied.

devanagari: ज्ञात्वा ज्ञातिवधं गर्ह्यमधर्मं राज्यहेतुकम् । ततो निवृत्तो हन्ताहं हतोऽयमिति लौकिकः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: jñātvā jñātivadhaṃ garhyamadharmaṃ rājyahetukamtato nivṛtto hantāhaṃ hato'yamiti laukikaḥ
translation: Knowing that killing one’s kinsfolk for the purpose of obtaining a kingdom is both censurable and irreligious, he, like a common man, came to believe (falsely) that he would be the killer and the other soul would be killed by him and he desisted from fighting.

devanagari: स तदा पुरुषव्याघ्रो युक्त्या मे प्रतिबोधितः । अभ्यभाषत मामेवं यथा त्वं रणमूर्धनि ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tadā puruṣavyāghro yuktyā me pratibodhitaḥabhyabhāṣata māmevaṃ yathā tvaṃ raṇamūrdhani
translation: At that time that liger-like (Valiant) hero who was made to realize (the correct philosophical position in the matter) by logical reasoning, spoke to me in the same manner at the battle-front, as you did now.

devanagari: अहमात्मोद्धवामीषां भूतानां सुहृदीश्वरः । अहं सर्वाणि भूतानि तेषां स्थित्युद्भवाप्ययः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ahamātmoddhavāmīṣāṃ bhūtānāṃ suhṛdīśvaraḥahaṃ sarvāṇi bhūtāni teṣāṃ sthityudbhavāpyayaḥ
translation: I am the (Indwelling) Soul, a friend and the Ruler of all these beings, O Uddhava. In fact I constitute all beings; I am the Creator, Protector and Destroyer of these beings.

devanagari: अहं गतिर्गतिमतां कालः कलयतामहम् । गुणानां चाप्यहं साम्यं गुणिन्यौत्पत्तिको गुणः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ gatirgatimatāṃ kālaḥ kalayatāmahamguṇānāṃ cāpyahaṃ sāmyaṃ guṇinyautpattiko guṇaḥ
translation: I am the motive force among the mobile; I am the Time-Spirit (Kāla) among those that bring other things under control. I am the state of equipoise of the guṇas and I constitute the property natural in things possessing properties.

devanagari: गुणिनामप्यहं सूत्रं महतां च महानहम् । सूक्ष्माणामप्यहं जीवो दुर्जयानामहं मनः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇināmapyahaṃ sūtraṃ mahatāṃ ca mahānahamsūkṣmāṇāmapyahaṃ jīvo durjayānāmahaṃ manaḥ
translation: I am the sūtra, the first evolute of Prakṛti—the active aspect in the products of guṇas; I am the greatness or the principle called Mahat among things with large dimensions. Among the subtlest of things, I am the vital Principle and among the invincibles, I am the mind.

devanagari: हिरण्यगर्भो वेदानां मन्त्राणां प्रणवस्त्रिवृत् । अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि पदानि च्छन्दुसामहम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyagarbho vedānāṃ mantrāṇāṃ praṇavastrivṛtakṣarāṇāmakāro'smi padāni cchandusāmaham
translation: Of the Vedas, I am god Brahmā (Hiraṇyagarbha who is the first teacher of the Vedas); of mantras, I am the Praṇava (Oṃ) constituted of the three syllabus (A U M); of the sounds represented by the letters of the alphabet, I am (the first sound) A; of all the metres, I am the Gāyatrī metre (consisting of three feet).

devanagari: इन्द्रो ऽहं सर्वदेवानां वसूनामस्मि हव्यवाट् । आदित्यानामहं विष्णू रुद्राणां नीललोहितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: indro 'haṃ sarvadevānāṃ vasūnāmasmi havyavāṭādityānāmahaṃ viṣṇū rudrāṇāṃ nīlalohitaḥ
translation: I am Indra out of all celestials and the fire-god among all the Vasus. Of the Sun-gods, I am Viṣṇu and out of the (eleven) Rudras, I am Lord Śiva.

devanagari: ब्रह्मर्षीणां भृगुरहं राजर्षीणामहं मनुः । देवर्षीणां नारदोऽहं हविर्धान्यस्मि धेनुषु ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmarṣīṇāṃ bhṛgurahaṃ rājarṣīṇāmahaṃ manuḥdevarṣīṇāṃ nārado'haṃ havirdhānyasmi dhenuṣu
translation: Out of Brāhmaṇa sages, I am Bhṛgu and out of royal sages I am Manu (the progenitor of the human race). I am Nārada out of celestial sages, and the wish-yielding cow, Havirclhānī amongst cows.

devanagari: सिद्धेश्वराणां कपिलः सुपर्णोऽहं पतत्रिणाम् । प्रजापतीनां दक्षोऽहं पितॄणामहमर्यमा ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: siddheśvarāṇāṃ kapilaḥ suparṇo'haṃ patatriṇāmprajāpatīnāṃ dakṣo'haṃ pitṝṇāmahamaryamā
translation: Out of masters of super-normal powers (siddhas), I am Kapila; out of birds, I am Garuḍa, I am Dakṣa out of Prajāpatis (The lords presiding over creation) and am Aryamā out of the manes or departed ancestors.

devanagari: मां विद्ध्युद्धव दैत्यानां प्रह्लादमसुरेश्वरम् । सोमं नक्षत्रौषधीनां धनेशं यक्षरक्षसाम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: māṃ viddhyuddhava daityānāṃ prahlādamasureśvaramsomaṃ nakṣatrauṣadhīnāṃ dhaneśaṃ yakṣarakṣasām
translation: O Uddhava! Know that I am Prahlāda, the king of Asuras among the demons (Daityas). Out of constellations and medicinal plants, I am Soma, and I am Kubera out of Yakṣas and Rākṣasas.

devanagari: ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां यादसां वरुणं प्रभुम् । तपतां द्युमतां सूर्यं मनुष्याणां च भूपतिम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: airāvataṃ gajendrāṇāṃ yādasāṃ varuṇaṃ prabhumtapatāṃ dyumatāṃ sūryaṃ manuṣyāṇāṃ ca bhūpatim
translation: Know me to be Airāvata (the elephant of Indra) among mighty elephants and Varuṇa, the Lord of acquatic animals. Know me to be the Sun among the heavenly luminous bodies emitting heat and light and the Lord of the terrestrial globe among human beings.

devanagari: उच्चैःश्रवास्तुरङ्गाणां धातूनामस्मि काञ्चनम् । यमः संयमतां चाहम्सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: uccaiḥśravāsturaṅgāṇāṃ dhātūnāmasmi kāñcanamyamaḥ saṃyamatāṃ cāhamsarpāṇāmasmi vāsukiḥ
translation: Out of horses, I am Uccaiḥśravas and gold out of (excavated) metals; out of deities dispensing justice and punishment. I am Yama (the god of Death) and out of serpents know that I am Vāsuki.

devanagari: नागेन्द्राणामनन्तोऽहं मृगेन्द्रः शृङ्गिदंष्ट्रिणाम् । आश्रमाणामहं तुर्यो वर्णानां प्रथमोऽनघ ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: nāgendrāṇāmananto'haṃ mṛgendraḥ śṛṅgidaṃṣṭriṇāmāśramāṇāmahaṃ turyo varṇānāṃ prathamo'nagha
translation: I am Lord Ananta (Śeṣa) out of the serpent-kings and a lion (lord of beasts) among animals with horns and fangs. Know me to be the fourth Āśrama, (sannyāsa) among the Āśramas (stages of life) and the first Varṇa (Brāhmaṇas) among the classes in society.

devanagari: तीर्थानां स्रोतसां गङ्गा समुद्र ः सरसामहम् । आयुधानां धनुरहं त्रिपुरघ्नो धनुष्मताम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tīrthānāṃ srotasāṃ gaṅgā samudra ḥ sarasāmahamāyudhānāṃ dhanurahaṃ tripuraghno dhanuṣmatām
translation: I am the Gaṅgā among sacred rivers and streams and the sea among all the reservoirs of water. I am the bow among weapons and god Śiva, the slayer of Tripura, among archers.

devanagari: धिष्ण्यानामस्म्यहं मेरुर्गहनानां हिमालयः । वनस्पतीनामश्वत्थ ओषधीनामहं यवः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: dhiṣṇyānāmasmyahaṃ merurgahanānāṃ himālayaḥvanaspatīnāmaśvattha oṣadhīnāmahaṃ yavaḥ
translation: Among all abodes, I am mount Meru (the abode of gods) and of all steep and inaccessible places, I am the Himālayas. Out of trees, I am the Pippal tree and I am barley of all the (food yielding) plants.

devanagari: पुरोधसां वसिष्ठोऽहं ब्रह्मिष्ठानां बृहस्पतिः । स्कन्दोऽहं सर्वसेनान्यामग्रण्यां भगवानजः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: purodhasāṃ vasiṣṭho'haṃ brahmiṣṭhānāṃ bṛhaspatiḥskando'haṃ sarvasenānyāmagraṇyāṃ bhagavānajaḥ
translation: Out of all sacrificial priests, I am Vasiṣṭha, and Bṛhaspati (the preceptor of gods) among the experts in the Vedas. Out of all the leaders of armies, know me to be Skanda and god Brahmā of all persons who show people the right path

devanagari: यज्ञानां ब्रह्मयज्ञोऽहं व्रतानामविहिंसनम् । वाय्वग्न्यर्काम्बुवागात्मा शुचीनामप्यहं शुचिः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yajñānāṃ brahmayajño'haṃ vratānāmavihiṃsanamvāyvagnyarkāmbuvāgātmā śucīnāmapyahaṃ śuciḥ
translation: Out of all sacrificial performances, I am Brahmayajña[3] (the recitation and teaching of Vedas) and non-violence (even by word) out of all sacred vows. Out of all purifying agents like air, fire, sun, water and speech (vāk), I am the purifying power—the sanctifier of all purifying agencies).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Which satisfies gods, sages, manes, living beings and the universe—EK

devanagari: योगानामात्मसंरोधो मन्त्रोऽस्मि विजिगीषताम् । आन्वीक्षिकी कौशलानां विकल्पः ख्यातिवादिनाम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yogānāmātmasaṃrodho mantro'smi vijigīṣatāmānvīkṣikī kauśalānāṃ vikalpaḥ khyātivādinām
translation: Out of the (eight) steps or limbs of yoga, I am the absorption of the Self (in Brahma) or Samādhi. I am political wisdom among those who are ambitious of conquests; among the scientific systems of thought, I am the scientific thought system that distinguishes between the matter and spirit (ātman and anātman). I am the insoluble doubt in the quest of the theories of perception of knowledge (known as Khyāti[4] as akhyāti of Mimāṃsakas, Śūnyakhyāti of Buddhists, asat-khyāti of Naiyāyikas, etc.).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
There are five Kḥhātis, each espoused by a different school of thought. They are enumerated below:
ātma-khyātir asatkhyātir akhyātiḥ khyātir anyathā /
tathā'nīrvacana-khyātir ityetat khyāti-pañcakam //
vijñāna-śūnyamīmāṃsā-tarkādvaita-vidāṃ matam.

devanagari: स्त्रीणां तु शतरूपाहं पुंसां स्वायम्भुवो मनुः । नारायणो मुनीनां च कुमारो ब्रह्मचारिणाम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: strīṇāṃ tu śatarūpāhaṃ puṃsāṃ svāyambhuvo manuḥnārāyaṇo munīnāṃ ca kumāro brahmacāriṇām
translation: I am Śatarūpā (the first woman, Svāyambhuva Manu’s wife) out of women, and Svāyambhuva Manu (the progenitor of the human race) out of men. Out of sages, I am Nārāyaṇa and (Sanat—) Kumāra among the observers of celebacy.

devanagari: धर्माणामस्मि सन्न्यासः क्षेमाणामबहिर्मतिः । गुह्यानां सुनृतं मौनं मिथुनानामजस्त्वहम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmāṇāmasmi sannyāsaḥ kṣemāṇāmabahirmatiḥguhyānāṃ sunṛtaṃ maunaṃ mithunānāmajastvaham
translation: Of all the duties of righteousness, I am Sannyāsa[5] (the assurance of protection against fear); of all causes conducive to security, I am the thought concentrated within (on the self). Of all the means of retaining secrets, I am sweet speech and silence. Of all couples, I am god Brahmā (who divided his person in man and woman).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Resigning all desire for the reward of all actions to the Lord ( anabhisaṃhita-phalo dharmaḥ)—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: संवत्सरोऽस्म्यनिमिषामृतूनां मधुमाधवौ । मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहं नक्षत्राणां तथाभिजित् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃvatsaro'smyanimiṣāmṛtūnāṃ madhumādhavaumāsānāṃ mārgaśīrṣo'haṃ nakṣatrāṇāṃ tathābhijit
translation: Of all the divisions of Time,[6] I am Saṃvatsara (a year) and of all seasons, I am the spring (consisting of the months of Caitra and Vaiśākha). Out of the months (of a year), I am Mārgaśīrṣa and out of all constellations of stars, I am Abhijit (that forms the last part of the asterism Uttarāṣāḍhā and the first of Śrāvaṇa).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Of all those who are vigilant (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā)

devanagari: अहं युगानां च कृतं धीराणां देवलोऽसितः । द्वैपायनोऽस्मि व्यासानां कवीनां काव्य आत्मवान् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ yugānāṃ ca kṛtaṃ dhīrāṇāṃ devalo'sitaḥdvaipāyano'smi vyāsānāṃ kavīnāṃ kāvya ātmavān
translation: Out of yugas, I am the Kṛta Age and of all intelligent bold thinkers,[7] I am Asita and Devala. I am Dvaipāyana (son of Parāśara and Satyavatī) among all the classifiers of the Vedas. Of wise politicians, I am the self-controlled Śukra.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Persons with fortitude to undergo pair of opposites like pleasure and pain—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: वासुदेवो भगवतां त्वं तु भागवतेष्वहम् । किम्पुरुषानां हनुमान्विद्याध्राणां सुदर्शनः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevo bhagavatāṃ tvaṃ tu bhāgavateṣvahamkimpuruṣānāṃ hanumānvidyādhrāṇāṃ sudarśanaḥ
translation: Among all the divine persons possessing the knowledge of creation and destruction of the universe and the individual and the spiritual wisdom and nescience[8], I am Vāsudeva while among the supreme devotees of the Lord, I am you (Uddhava).[9] Of all the Kimpuruṣas (demi-gods similar to monkeys) I am Hanumān, while among all the Vidyādharas I am Sudarśana.[10]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives the definition of Bhagavān as follows:
utpattiṃ pralayaṃ caiva bhūtānām agatiṃ gatim /
vetti vidyām avidyāṃ ca sa vācyo bhagavān iti /l
But Bhāgavata Candrikā and Padaratnāvalī follow the usual description of being endowed with six excellences like jñāna, aisvarya [aiśvarya?], etc.

Footnote [9]:
With Padaratnāvalī the seniority of the best devotees is as follows: Bhīma, Draupadī, Balarāma, Arjuna and lastly Uddhava.

Footnote [10]:
vide Supra X. 34.12-18.

devanagari: रत्नानां पद्मरागोऽस्मि पद्मकोशः सुपेशसाम् । कुशोऽस्मि दर्भजातीनां गव्यमाज्यं हविःष्वहम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ratnānāṃ padmarāgo'smi padmakośaḥ supeśasāmkuśo'smi darbhajātīnāṃ gavyamājyaṃ haviḥṣvaham
translation: I am the ruby out of all precious stones; I am the lotus-bud of all delicate and lovely objects. Of the grass of the darbha-type I am Kuśa. Out of all oblations to fire, I am butter made from cow’s milk.

devanagari: व्यवसायिनामहं लक्ष्मीः कितवानां छलग्रहः । तितिक्षास्मि तितिक्षूणां सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vyavasāyināmahaṃ lakṣmīḥ kitavānāṃ chalagrahaḥtitikṣāsmi titikṣūṇāṃ sattvaṃ sattvavatāmaham
translation: I am the wealth of the industrialists and businessmen (or those who strive to make money). Among all the gamblers, I am fraudulence. I am fortitude among the endurant persons and the energy among the energetic strivers.

devanagari: ओजः सहो बलवतां कर्माहं विद्धि सात्वताम् । सात्वतां नवमूर्तीनामादिमूर्तिरहं परा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ojaḥ saho balavatāṃ karmāhaṃ viddhi sātvatāmsātvatāṃ navamūrtīnāmādimūrtirahaṃ parā
translation: I am (physical and mental) virility and might among the strong and I am desireless action (of devoted service) among the Sātvatas (the votaries of the Lord). I am the first Supreme manifestation (viz. Vāsudeva) among all the nine manifestations[11] (Vyūhas of the Lord worshipped by followers of the Pāñcarātra Āgama.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Bālaprabodhini enumerates them as follows: Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Nārāyaṇa, Hayagrīva, Varāha, Nṛsiṃha and Vāmana
Bhāgavata Candrikā (as can be deduced from his predecessor SS) is off the mark to enumerate the usual ten Avatāras of Viṣṇu, deleting Kalki from the list.
Padaratnāvalī’s list as usual is unique.

devanagari: विश्वावसुः पूर्वचित्तिर्गन्धर्वाप्सरसामहम् । भूधराणामहं स्थैर्यं गन्धमात्रमहं भुवः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: viśvāvasuḥ pūrvacittirgandharvāpsarasāmahambhūdharāṇāmahaṃ sthairyaṃ gandhamātramahaṃ bhuvaḥ
translation: Out of Gandharvas, I am Viśvāvasu and am Pūrvacitti[12] out of celestial damsels. I am stability among the mountains and the property of scent of the earth.

devanagari: अपां रसश्च परमस्तेजिष्ठानां विभावसुः । प्रभा सूर्येन्दुताराणां शब्दोऽहं नभसः परः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: apāṃ rasaśca paramastejiṣṭhānāṃ vibhāvasuḥprabhā sūryendutārāṇāṃ śabdo'haṃ nabhasaḥ paraḥ
translation: I am the taste among water (liquids). I am the Sun among the bright luminaries; I am the lustre of the Sun, the moon and the stars. I am the sound (the essential characteristic) of the sky.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यानां बलिरहं वीराणामहमर्जुनः । भूतानां स्थितिरुत्पत्तिरहं वै प्रतिसङ्क्रमः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyānāṃ balirahaṃ vīrāṇāmahamarjunaḥbhūtānāṃ sthitirutpattirahaṃ vai pratisaṅkramaḥ
translation: Of all those who are friendly to Brāhmaṇas, I am Bali and I am Arjuna among the warriors. I constitute the creation, sustenance and destruction of all the beings or elements).

devanagari: गत्युक्त्युत्सर्गोपादानमानन्दस्पर्शलक्षणम् । आस्वादश्रुत्यवघ्राणमहं सर्वेन्द्रियेन्द्रियम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: gatyuktyutsargopādānamānandasparśalakṣaṇamāsvādaśrutyavaghrāṇamahaṃ sarvendriyendriyam
translation: I am the functions of the sense-organs such as locomotion, expression, excretion, receiving and enjoyment as well as touching, tasting, hearing and smelling. I am the sense of all senses (i.e. the inherent power to perform these functions).

devanagari: पृथिवी वायुराकाश आपो ज्योतिरहं महान् । विकारः पुरुषोऽव्यक्तं रजः सत्त्वं तमः परम् । अहमेतत्प्रसङ्ख्यानं ज्ञानं तत्त्वविनिश्चयः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthivī vāyurākāśa āpo jyotirahaṃ mahānvikāraḥ puruṣo'vyaktaṃ rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamaḥ paramahametatprasaṅkhyānaṃ jñānaṃ tattvaviniścayaḥ
translation: I constitute the seven principles which are of the nature of both the cause and the effect, viz. the subtle elements—tanmātrās—of earth, air, sky, water, fire and Ego (Ahaṅkāra) and Mahat; I am also the (sixteen) modifications (which are only the effects and not cause, viz. five gross elements, ten cognitive and conative sense-organs and the mind), the individual soul (who is above causal relation) and the Unmanifest (Prakṛti which is only the cause of all) and (the three modifications of Prakṛti, viz.) Sattva, Rajas and Tamas[13] as well as the Transcendental Supreme Brahman.[14]
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the following Sāṅkhya Kārikā by Īśvara Kṛṣṇa bringing out the implication of this verse:
mūla-prakṛtir avikṛti-mahadādyāḥ prakṛti-vikṛtayaḥ sapta /
ṣoḍaśakaś [?ca?] vikāraḥ, na prakṛtir na vikṛtiḥ puruṣaḥ //

Footnote [14]:
Param—But I am distinct from them as I am the controller of those principles—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: मयेश्वरेण जीवेन गुणेन गुणिना विना । सर्वात्मनापि सर्वेण न भावो विद्यते क्वचित् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mayeśvareṇa jīvena guṇena guṇinā vināsarvātmanāpi sarveṇa na bhāvo vidyate kvacit
translation: I form the aggregate of these (Twenty-five principles) the knowledge (of their characteristics) as well as the consequent realization of the definite Truth. Nothing exists without my presence anywhere, as I am both the Supreme Ruler as well as individual soul, the modifications (of Prakṛti) or guṇas as well as the products of these guṇas.[15]
purport:
Footnote [15]:
I constitute the duality known as God and the individual soul, rhe guṇas and their products, the Inner Controller of the body as well as the body—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: सङ्ख्यानं परमाणूनां कालेन क्रियते मया । न तथा मे विभूतीनां सृजतोऽण्डानि कोटिशः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkhyānaṃ paramāṇūnāṃ kālena kriyate mayāna tathā me vibhūtīnāṃ sṛjato'ṇḍāni koṭiśaḥ
translation: The (impossible task of) taking a count of all atoms can be done by me in course of time, but the reckoning of all my manifestations is impossible as millions and millions of universes are continuously being created by me.

devanagari: तेजः श्रीः कीर्तिरैश्वर्यं ह्रीस्त्यागः सौभगं भगः । वीर्यं तितिक्षा विज्ञानं यत्र यत्र स मेंऽशकः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tejaḥ śrīḥ kīrtiraiśvaryaṃ hrīstyāgaḥ saubhagaṃ bhagaḥvīryaṃ titikṣā vijñānaṃ yatra yatra sa meṃ'śakaḥ
translation: Wherever there is majesty, affluence and celebrity, authority, modesty, liberality or beauty, fortune, capability, forbearance and precise knowledge of the Truth, there exists my part-manifestation (aṃśa).

devanagari: एतास्ते कीर्तिताः सर्वाः सङ्क्षेपेण विभूतयः । मनोविकारा एवैते यथा वाचाभिधीयते ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: etāste kīrtitāḥ sarvāḥ saṅkṣepeṇa vibhūtayaḥmanovikārā evaite yathā vācābhidhīyate
translation: All these manifestations of mine have been briefly described to you. But these are nothing but freaks of imagination (like the unreal sky-flower) which are only verbally expressed.[16]
purport:
Footnote [16]:
They are produced directly by my will power and are conveyed by the Vedas—
(mat-saṅkalpa-mātra-janyāḥ vācā vedena)—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: वाचं यच्छ मनो यच्छ प्राणान्यच्छेन्द्रियाणि च । आत्मानमात्मना यच्छ न भूयः कल्पसेऽध्वने ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: vācaṃ yaccha mano yaccha prāṇānyacchendriyāṇi caātmānamātmanā yaccha na bhūyaḥ kalpase'dhvane
translation: (Hence) control your speech; rest ain your mind, practise breath-control, subdue your sense-organs, bring your intellect under control by your (sāttvika) intellect so that you will not be liable to return to the path of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: यो वै वाङ्मनसी संयगसंयच्छन्धिया यतिः । तस्य व्रतं तपो दानं स्रवत्यामघटाम्बुवत् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: yo vai vāṅmanasī saṃyagasaṃyacchandhiyā yatiḥtasya vrataṃ tapo dānaṃ sravatyāmaghaṭāmbuvat
translation: The recluse (striver on the spiritual path) who does not strictly control his speech and mind with his reasoning power, finds that his sacred vows, austere penance and liberality in gifts leak away like water from an unbaked jar.

devanagari: तस्माद्वचो मनः प्राणान्नियच्छेन्मत्परायणः । मद्भक्तियुक्तया बुद्ध्या ततः परिसमाप्यते ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādvaco manaḥ prāṇānniyacchenmatparāyaṇaḥmadbhaktiyuktayā buddhyā tataḥ parisamāpyate
translation:
Therefore, devoting himself solely and unflinchingly to me and with his intellect full of devotion to me, he should control his mind, speech and breath. It is then alone that he attains Liberation from Saṃsāra—which is the end of the journey.
[back to top]
